$comment: Verify-policy record for one pilot — manual, file-based (no config service). This documents, in one place, the policy the pilot's verifier enforces. It is NOT loaded by any service: each field says where the live value actually lives. Copy next to the partner file as pilot/partners/<label>.policy.json.

verifier.$kinds: classical = hosted POST /v1/verify (no ZK proof verification; trust anchor is the operator key set). zk = partner runs `bolyra verify` locally (full Groth16 + vkey pinning). See spec/external-verifier-contract-v1.md §3.5.

$trustedOperators_live: integrations/hosted-verify/wrangler.jsonc TRUSTED_OPERATORS (redeploy to change). For the CLI path: the partner's --trusted-operators configuration.
$capabilityMap_live: Same shape as `bolyra verify --capability-map` and the Worker's optional CAPABILITY_MAP var (merged over the built-in default). Unmapped capabilities are ALWAYS denied (unknown_capability, fail-closed).
$nonceMode_note: The hosted Worker is stateless: always host mode — the partner MUST reserve every consume_nonces entry in durable storage before acting (reserve-before-act, spec §7.3). Local mode replay state is available only via the `bolyra verify` CLI.

receipts.$note: issuer/keyId live in wrangler.jsonc; the signing key is the RECEIPT_SIGNER_KEY Worker secret. Record the signer ADDRESS here so the partner can pass --signer to `bolyra receipt verify-chain` — never the key.

expiry.$note: Credential expiry is signature-bound (binding v2): the operator-signed binding pins expiry to the revealed credential expiry. now_unix >= expiry denies with code=expired (strict; equality is expired).
